raw_consumer_statement:
I am a 3-year American Express customer with zero prior disputes. I filed 9 fraud disputes totaling $ XXXX for Amex charges against merchant XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX involving multiple properties I paid for but never received ownership of. 

INCONSISTENT TREATMENT : American Express approved 1 dispute ( Ref : XXXX, {$1500.00} ) confirming fraud occurred. However, they denied 8 identical disputes citing 'past 60 days ' - despite all 9 involving the same merchant and identical fraud scheme. 

FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT APPLIES : Merchant actively deceived me through XX/XX/XXXX when I texted asking about reimbursement and he made false promises. I filed disputes XX/XX/XXXX - just 9 days after last deceptive contact. Under fraudulent concealment doctrine, the filing timeframe starts from the last deceptive act or discovery, not original transaction dates. 

IRREFUTABLE EVIDENCE - MULTIPLE PROPERTIES FRAUD : Merchant 's own text messages admit systematic fraud : 'You bought properties and the deeds are paid for just wait ' ( admitting I paid for multiple properties ) 'You got the deed for the XXXX property, The others I still have to transfer ' ( admitting multiple properties exist, never transferred 'the others ' ) PRIMARY PROPERTY ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, AL XXXX : XXXX XXXX property records prove merchant 's LLC XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) owns this property ( acquired XX/XX/XXXX ) I paid merchant for this property in XXXX Merchant NEVER transferred ownership to me - NO DEED from XXXX XXXX XXXX to me exists in county records XXXX XXXX records show merchant 's LLC STILL OWNS property as of XX/XX/XXXX Merchant provided fraudulent quitclaim deed that was never notarized or recorded ( legally invalid ) ADDITIONAL PROPERTY : I was buying second property through merchant Merchant 's text admits 'the others I still have to transfer " Never received deed for this property Never received any ownership transfer Never heard about it again after payment TOTAL FRAUD SCHEME : Merchant 's LLC owned at least one property, I paid for multiple properties through him totaling $ XXXX ( Amex charges ), merchant took my money but never transferred ownership of any properties. County records prove merchant retained ownership while taking payment - classic fraud documented by official records and merchant 's own written admissions. 

SYSTEM FAILURE : American Express 's automated system repeatedly denies these disputes without human fraud investigation review. Even when reopened by representatives, the system auto-denies before fraud investigators can see evidence including county property records, fraudulent deed, and merchant 's text confessions.
